prompt:
Mail archiving squad here. Our compliance exporter walks ~18 Outlook folders per mailbox with one HTTP round-trip per folder per page and it takes forever; worse, when Graph throttles ONE folder the whole run starts over and re-downloads everything. I want a proper JSON-batch client in TypeScript for Node — no npm deps, node:test is the harness.

Do the homework first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Microsoft Graph docs on learn.microsoft.com — the JSON batching concept page, the throttling guidance (it has a specific section about throttling INSIDE batches), and the mailFolder list-messages v1.0 reference. Confirm the $batch endpoint and body schema, the 20-request limit, how subresponses are ordered (they aren't), and what a throttled subrequest looks like. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both protected. If live docs contradict the fixture, flag it in your summary and leave fixtures/tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

Deliverable: batch_mail_client.ts as the entry point (split internals — batch envelope building, correlation, retry policy — into more files as you see fit), exporting GraphBatchClient and GraphBatchError.

new GraphBatchClient({baseUrl, accessToken, delay, maxRetries}) — baseUrl like https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0 (tests point it at a loopback fake), delay(seconds) injectable (never real timers when provided), maxRetries defaults to 3.

fetchMail(userId, folderIds) → per-folder reports in INPUT order: {id, displayName, totalItemCount, messages, error}. Each folder costs two subrequests — metadata (?$select=id,displayName,totalItemCount) and messages (?$select=id,subject,from,receivedDateTime,isRead&$top=5). Messages map to {id, subject, from (the address string out of from.emailAddress), receivedDateTime, isRead}.

Rules the tests enforce (docs/contract.json spells them out):
- Everything goes through POST {base}/$batch — relative subrequest urls, unique string ids, ≤20 per batch and FILL them (36 subrequests = batches of 20 then 16), batches issued sequentially. The fake serves nothing but $batch, so a bare GET anywhere is an instant failure.
- Correlate subresponses strictly by id — the fake deliberately returns them in reverse order.
- A messages page can carry @odata.nextLink. Follow it via $batch: strip exactly the version base off the absolute link and reuse path+query BYTE-FOR-BYTE (the fake 400s any rebuilt token). Group all due continuations into as few batches as possible.
- Throttling: a subresponse with status 429 carries Retry-After (seconds) in its headers. Wait the LONGEST Retry-After among that round's throttled subrequests via the injected delay, then retry ONLY those subrequests in a fresh batch. Successful siblings are kept, never re-requested. After maxRetries rounds a folder reports error {status: 429, code: 'TooManyRequests', retryAfter} but keeps whatever it already got (metadata, earlier pages).
- Non-429 subresponse errors (404 ErrorItemNotFound etc.) are terminal per folder: {status, code, message} on that folder, no retry, siblings unaffected.
- Batch-level failures (401 etc.) throw GraphBatchError with status/code. The access token must never appear in any report or error text.

Acceptance: node --test test_batch_mail.ts until green. test_batch_mail.ts and docs/ are protected.

research.product: Microsoft Graph JSON batching over Outlook mail (mailFolders/messages)
research.interface: Microsoft Graph REST API v1.0, POST /$batch (https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/$batch)

official_sources.title: Combine multiple HTTP requests using JSON batching
official_sources.used_for: POST /v1.0/$batch endpoint; requests[] schema (id/method/url/headers/body) with unique ids (duplicate = 400), relative resource URLs, Content-Type required only when a body is present; responses[] schema (id/status/headers/body) explicitly unordered, correlated by id; batch-level 200 independent of per-subrequest status; 20-request batch size limit

official_sources.title: Microsoft Graph throttling guidance
official_sources.used_for: requests inside a batch are throttled individually: the batch returns 200 while throttled subrequests carry status 429 with a retry-after value in the subresponse; the documented recovery is to retry only the failed subrequests in a new batch after the longest retry-after; SDK auto-retry does not apply to batched requests

official_sources.title: List messages - Microsoft Graph v1.0
official_sources.used_for: GET /users/{id}/mailFolders/{id}/messages request path, Authorization Bearer header, OData query parameter support ($select/$top), 200 OK message collection with subject/receivedDateTime/from properties

official_sources.title: Paging Microsoft Graph data in your app
official_sources.used_for: @odata.nextLink must be used as returned until it stops appearing; the $skiptoken/$skip inside it is opaque and must not be extracted or modified; a page may contain zero results; original query parameters are already encoded into the nextLink

research.language_rationale: The exporter feeds a Node-based mail archiving service, and Graph's JSON batching is a plain REST envelope with no special SDK semantics; TypeScript on Node's built-in fetch/http stack (node:test harness, zero npm dependencies) is the idiomatic way to build and pin the raw $batch contract.
